title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins filed to run for Suffolk County District Attorney, a position she held from 2019 to 2021.
Rollins resigned as U.S. Attorney in 2023 after a Justice Department probe into her ethics and conduct.
Current Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion also filed paperwork to run for the position.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford Firefighter Nick Spinale critically injured after a 40-foot fall during training at Massachusetts Firefighting Academy.
Spinale, 28, sustained severe injuries and requires multiple surgeries at UMass Memorial Medical Center in Worcester.
Chelmsford Firefighters Local 1839 requests community support and acknowledges immediate care provided by academy staff and EMS.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox players have adopted a new celebration inspired by a TikTok dance video from outfielder Roman Anthony.
The dance involves gestures toward the dugout and originates from Anthony's high-school TikTok performance of Mariah Carey’s 'Obsessed.'
Boston achieved their first series win of the season against the Brewers, improving to a 4-8 record under manager Alex Cora.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kevin Scott Kelley, former Adams police chief, indicted for fraudulent overtime claims worth nearly $20,000.
Charges include procurement fraud, false claims, and larceny over $1200; arraignment set for May 4.
Kelley allegedly claimed non-worked overtime related to a grant administered by the Massachusetts Department of Transportation.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions passed a no confidence vote, seeking Chief Charles Gray's removal for leadership failures.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons's recent acquittal highlighted issues in the department, including the lack of body-worn cameras.
Police Chief Gray has ignored recommendations from a 2020 management review, worsening trust and morale within the department.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics reportedly interested in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo amid Milwaukee Bucks' struggles.
Antetokounmpo under contract through 2028, with potential free-agency option after next season.
Celtics face financial challenges pairing Antetokounmpo with Tatum and Brown under luxury tax constraints.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Milwaukee Brewers 5-0, securing their first series win of the season at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched 6 1/3 scoreless innings, lowering his ERA to 2.76, and received praises from manager Alex Cora.
Tyler Samaniego made his MLB debut, striking out the side in a scoreless eighth, honoring his late father with an inscribed glove.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget with a 2.1% spending increase for fiscal year 2027.
Rising healthcare costs and police overtime contribute to a $48.4 million budget deficit for Boston.
The budget includes cuts to discretionary grant programs, delaying hiring, and freezing some spending to manage costs.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame.
Gronkowski is a first-year eligible nominee with 80 touchdowns, second in Patriots receiving yards.
The fan vote ends April 28; results will be announced the following week.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed top prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract, starting immediately for the 2025-26 season.
James Hagens, 19, impressed with his skillset during six games with the Providence Bruins, scoring one goal and three assists.
Joining the Bruins' roster, Hagens is set to contribute to their playoff push, potentially improving the third line and power play.
